category.name: Watermark
category.preferred_workflow: Use simple documented watermark workflows. Prefer Document.Watermark APIs when they directly match the task. For lower-level or cell-scoped scenarios, use compile-safe shape or image placement only when needed. Keep validation narrow and task-specific.

core_rules: Create local sample source documents or images when a task refers to an existing file, folder, stream, template, or input asset.
Do not assume external files or folders already exist.
Always save the requested output document or report when the task expects one.
watermark_rules: Use Document.Watermark.SetText and SetImage when those APIs directly fit the task.
Avoid unsupported System.Drawing-based image generation in this environment.
For cell-level or merged-cell scenarios, use compile-safe shape or image insertion patterns only.
Use stream-based or local-file image workflows for image watermarks.
api_safety_rules: Do not invent unsupported watermark APIs.
Do not use unsupported WordArt or TextPath members in cell-level watermark tasks.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Generated code references members that do not exist in the selected package version.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Replace invented members with documented Aspose.Words APIs already proven in this category.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: The example assumes source files, folders, images, or data already exist.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create deterministic local inputs before loading, processing, or validating them.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: The example fails at runtime while checking unrelated document internals.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Validate only the requested behavior and the existence of expected outputs.

examples.task: Load a Word document from a file path and add a text watermark using Watermark.SetText.
Load a Word document from a file path and add an image watermark using Watermark.SetImage.
Load a Word document from a memory stream and apply a text watermark without writing to disk.
Insert a text watermark into a specific table cell within a Word document using the Watermark class.
Add a watermark to a table cell that spans multiple rows and columns in a complex Word table.
Insert a watermark into a table cell that contains merged cells without disrupting the table layout.
Insert a watermark into each cell of the first row of a table using the Watermark class.
Add a text watermark to a DOCX document using Watermark.SetText with custom font settings.
Use Watermark.SetText with TextWatermarkOptions to set watermark font size, color, and spacing.
Add a confidential text watermark to all new documents created by an automated report generator.
Combine text and image watermarks by first setting a text watermark then overlaying an image watermark.
Customize image watermark opacity and scaling by configuring ImageWatermarkOptions before insertion.
Insert an image watermark from a System.Drawing.Image object into a document after calling Optimize.
Insert an image watermark from a file path into a Word document after optimizing the document.
Use Watermark.SetImage with a stream to embed a logo watermark into a document stored in Azure Blob storage.
Use Watermark.SetImage with a byte array stream to embed a dynamically generated barcode watermark.
Optimize a large DOCX file before applying an image watermark to improve performance and memory usage.
Remove all existing watermarks from a loaded Word document using the Watermark.Remove method.
Create a utility method that removes all watermarks from a Document using Watermark.Remove.
Validate that a document contains no watermarks before publishing by using WatermarkType.None check.
Use WatermarkType enumeration to verify a document has no watermark before adding a new one.
Use WatermarkType enumeration to switch between text and image watermarks based on user selection.
Save a watermarked Word document directly to PDF format while preserving watermark appearance.
Apply a text watermark to a Word document and then save the document as DOCX.
Apply an image watermark to a Word document and then save the document as DOCX.
Batch process a folder of DOC files to add the same image watermark to each document.
Batch process multiple Word documents in a directory to add a text watermark to each file.
Batch process multiple Word documents in a directory to remove existing watermarks from each file.
Batch convert DOCX files to PDF while adding a corporate logo image watermark to each PDF.
Create a command‑line tool that accepts a directory path and adds a specified watermark to each file.
Use a configuration file to define watermark text, font, and opacity, then apply it to multiple documents.
Implement a unit test that verifies Watermark.Remove successfully deletes a previously added text watermark.
Add a watermark to a document opened from a network share, ensuring proper disposal of file handles.
Create a reusable method that adds a configurable text watermark to any Document object.
